title: West Asia conflict: How an Indian in UAE opened the doors of his farm for stranded Indians
summary: The initiative was organised by Dhiraj Jain, a businessman based in the UAE, who offered his property to those left stranded

title: International Women’s Day: Educated, empowered women pillars of progressive nation, says Droupadi Murmu
summary: As 'Nari Shakti' continues to excel across diverse fields and lead with courage, it strengthens the foundations of a more inclusive and prosperous society, she said

title: In Chennai, a former Commissioner’s name lives on, in different forms
summary: Nearly ten years since historian V. Sriram pointed out that two road name boards would cause Sir Eric Conran-Smith to turn in his grave, those twin errors persist

title: Mercury rises across north; Delhi records highest maximum temperature for first week of March in 50 years
summary: According to weather data from the past 50 years, earlier the highest maximum temperature reading during the first seven days of March at Safdarjung, the city's base weather station, was 34.8°C logged on March 5, 1999.

title: 45% Kashmir students up to Class 12 report sub-optimal mental well-being due to social media: study
summary: “The inability of some students to check in with some technologies according to their wishes results in a feeling of anxiety. More than 14% were moderately to highly anxious when they were unable to use text messages,” according to the study, published this year by the Ianna Journal of Interdisciplinary Studies

title: Delhi court grants two-week interim bail to Al-Falah chairman Jawed Siddiqui in money laundering case
summary: “After considering all the present facts and circumstances, I am of the considered view that the applicant/ accused deserves to be enlarged on interim bail as the wife of applicant/ accused is unwell,” the court said in its order

title: Once trapped in bonded labour, now stitching a livelihood of her own
summary: Once bonded to work in sugarcane fields, Valli is now a tailor with an independent means of income

title: Why India’s ‘leaky pipeline’ in research is unlike the rest of the world
summary: Most women PhD holders in STEM in India find themselves unable to access long-term, lucrative, and prestigious research jobs
